outputs.Why is the sky blue?: The sun sends light in all colors. Our eyes see blue light best. Blue light scatters more in the air. That’s why the sky looks blue. When the sun is high, the sky is blue. When the sun is low, the sky is orange or red. The blue light goes all around the world. That’s why the sky is blue every day.
outputs.How do plants make their own food?: Plants make their own food using sunlight. They have something called chlorophyll in their leaves. Chlorophyll helps them catch the sunlight. The plant also needs water and air. Water comes from the soil. Air comes from the air around the plant. The plant uses all these things to make food. This process is called photosynthesis.
outputs.Why do we have day and night?: The Earth spins around like a top. This spinning makes different parts of the Earth face the Sun. When a part faces the Sun, it's daytime. When it turns away, it's nighttime. The Sun only shines on one side of the Earth at a time. So day and night happen because the Earth is spinning.
outputs.What makes ice melt?: Ice melts because it gets warm. When the sun shines on ice, it sends heat. The heat makes the ice get hotter. Hotter ice changes from solid to liquid. That's why ice turns into water. The water flows down and makes a puddle. Ice melts when it's not cold anymore. The warmth from the sun or your hand can make ice melt.
outputs.How do magnets work?: Magnets have special powers that make things stick. They have something called poles, like a north and a south. When you bring two magnets close, they either pull or push each other. The pull happens when the poles are opposite. The push happens when the poles are the same. You can feel the force of a magnet even if you can't see it. Magnets can make metal things move without touching them. That's how they work!
outputs.Why do things fall to the ground?: Things fall to the ground because of gravity. Gravity is a force that pulls everything toward the Earth. The Earth is big and heavy, so it pulls things down. When you drop something, the Earth's gravity makes it come down. This is why apples fall from trees and balls drop when you throw them up. Gravity helps keep us on the ground and keeps the moon going around the Earth. Without gravity, things would float away into space.
outputs.Where does a puddle go when it dries up?: When a puddle dries up, it turns into a dry spot. The water in the puddle evaporates. Evaporation means the water turns into air. The sun helps the water turn into air. The air carries the water away. Sometimes, the water might mix with the ground. But most of it goes up into the sky. The sky is full of air. The water is no longer in the puddle. It’s now in the air or in the ground.
outputs.Why do we have seasons?: The Earth goes around the Sun. As it does, it tilts on its axis. This tilt makes some parts of the Earth get more sunlight in summer. Other parts get less sunlight in winter. The seasons change because of this tilt. So, when it's warm in one place, it's cold in another. That's why we have different seasons.
outputs.How do our lungs help us breathe?: Our lungs are in our chest. They help us breathe by letting air in. When we breathe in, air goes through our nose or mouth. The air goes to the lungs. The lungs have tiny balloons called alveoli. These balloons help take in oxygen from the air. The oxygen goes to our blood. The blood carries the oxygen to our body. The lungs also help get carbon dioxide out of our body. We breathe out the carbon dioxide.
outputs.What makes a rainbow?: A rainbow happens when sunlight shines through raindrops. The sunlight splits into different colors. Each color bends slightly as it goes through a raindrop. The raindrops catch the light and send it back to the sky. You see the colors because they spread out in different directions. The rain has to be in the air, and the sun has to be behind you. That’s why you see a rainbow after it rains.
outputs.Why does the moon look like it changes s: The moon looks like it changes shape because it is not a solid ball. It is a round rock that orbits Earth. As it moves around Earth, different parts of the moon face the sun. The part that faces the sun shines bright. The part that does not face the sun is dark. So, depending on where the moon is, you see different shapes. That’s why the moon looks like it changes.
outputs.How do fish breathe underwater?: Fish have gills to help them breathe underwater. Gills are special parts in their head. Water flows through the gills slowly. The gills take in oxygen from the water. Oxygen is needed for the fish to live. The fish use the oxygen to stay alive. They get rid of the extra water and carbon dioxide.
